title: Eria agathodaemonis
intro: Eria agathodaemonis, also known as Agathodaemon Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eria agathodaemonis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eria agathodaemonis is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and tonics.
taxonomy: Eria agathodaemonis is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats in India. The classification of Eria agathodaemonis is as follows: it belongs to the genus Eria, the subtribe Coelogyninae, and tribe Coelogyneae. The family Orchidaceae is further classified under the order Asparagales and the class Liliopsida. Further research is needed to confirm its complete taxonomic classification.
